evals.prompt: Write the HTML for a page header with a skip link, a single main navigation region, and a current-page indicator on the Home link.
evals.expected_output: Uses a first-focusable skip link to #main, wraps primary navigation in <nav aria-label="Main">, uses <main id="main" tabindex="-1">, and marks the current page with aria-current="page".
expectations: Skip link appears before the rest of the page content
Primary navigation uses a <nav> landmark with a unique aria-label
The main landmark can receive programmatic focus via tabindex="-1"
The current page link is identified with aria-current="page"

evals.prompt: Refactor a site menu that currently uses role="menu" and role="menuitem" into semantic navigation.
evals.expected_output: Uses a <nav> landmark with a list of links, avoids menu roles, and keeps standard Tab navigation.
expectations: Uses native links inside a list
Does not use role="menu" for site navigation
Does not use role="menuitem" for ordinary navigation links
Preserves normal keyboard tab order

evals.prompt: Write breadcrumb markup for a product detail page.
evals.expected_output: Uses nav aria-label="Breadcrumb", an ordered list, and aria-current="page" on the final item.
expectations: Breadcrumbs are wrapped in a labeled <nav> landmark
Breadcrumbs use an ordered list
The current page is marked with aria-current="page"
The breadcrumb label is short and descriptive

evals.prompt: Build a disclosure-style dropdown navigation for a Services section.
evals.expected_output: Uses a button with aria-expanded and aria-controls, keeps the submenu hidden until expanded, and does not use role="menu".
expectations: Top-level navigation remains semantic links or a disclosure button
The toggle button exposes state with aria-expanded
The toggle button references the submenu with aria-controls
The submenu is hidden until the disclosure is opened

evals.prompt: Write secondary footer navigation for policy links.
evals.expected_output: Uses a unique aria-label such as "Footer", wraps links in <nav>, and keeps link text visible and descriptive.
expectations: Secondary navigation has its own unique label
Links remain visible text rather than icon-only controls
The navigation is wrapped in a <nav> landmark
The label distinguishes the region from primary navigation
